mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer confidence and discretionary spending, which depresses equity returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a simultaneous drop in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 365369M and HOUST at 1933K in July 2011
regime_hint: demand-side recession risk or double-dip scenario
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously by specified thresholds, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a consistent month-over-month growth rate above 0.3%, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as a leading indicator of earnings momentum.
regime_hint: any regime with positive industrial momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns is negative or below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth does not precede positive returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 net new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains above 4.5%, signaling labor market stagnation that constrains consumer income and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134564K and UNRATE at 4.7% in July 2011
regime_hint: weak labor market recovery with stalled job creation
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS growth < 50K and UNRATE > 4.5% occur simultaneously, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, creating a negative real interest rate that erodes bond returns and forces equity risk premium compression, producing negative 20-day forward returns as investors rotate away from equities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.7 and FEDFUNDS at 4.29% in July 2011
failure_condition: When negative real rates (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < 3.0%) occur, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling late-cycle slowdown and recession risk, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors price in earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% in most recent quarter
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously contracts, indicating synchronized supply and labor weakness that signals recession onset, producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.269 and PAYEMS at 134564K in July 2011
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously by specified thresholds, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.20).
